DanishWriting:
... Tak for sidst. Du fortalte, at I skulle have en hundehvalp. Hvordan går det med den?

Vi har besluttet os for at købe en ladcykel, men den er ret dyr. Vi ved ikke, om vi skal låne pengene, så vi kan købe den nu, eller vi skal vente, til har sparet op. Hvad ville du gøre?

Når vi har købt en ladcykel, behøver vi måske slet ikke bilen mere. Jeg bor jo tæt på mit arbejde, og det samme gør Mette. Tror du; vi kan undvære den?

Vi overvejer også at droppe at rejse med fly i sommerferien og blive i Danmark. Du lejede jo et sommerhus sidste år. Tror du, det vil være noget for os? ...

----------------------------------------------------------------

Kære Peter,

Tak for din mail. Det var rigtig hyggeligt at se jer sidst. Med hensyn til vores hundehvalp går det over al forventning. Den vokser hurtigt og er allerede faldet godt til i vores rammer.

Hvor lyder det spændende med ladcyklen! Hvis jeg var i dit sted, ville jeg helt klart vælge at vente med at købe den, til I har sparet pengene op. Selvom det kan være fristende at låne, er det altid rarest at undgå unødig gæld.

Hvad angår bilen, er jeg fuldstændig overbevist om, at I sagtens kan undvære den, eftersom I begge bor tæt på jeres arbejde. En ladcykel er ideel til hverdagens indkøb og transport, så I vil helt sikkert spare mange penge på forsikring og brændstof.

Mht. jeres sommerferie, tror jeg bestemt, at et sommerhus i Danmark vil være noget for jer. Det sommerhus, vi lejede sidste år, lå i et utrolig naturskønt område, hvor der var rig mulighed for at slappe af. Det kan varmt anbefales, især hvis I ønsker en stressfri ferie.

Hvad siger du til, at vi mødes i næste uge over en kop kaffe? Så kan vi vende det hele overgribende, og jeg kan vise dig nogle billeder fra sommerhuset.

De bedste hilsner,
Emre
EnglishWriting:
... Thanks for last time. You mentioned that you were getting a puppy. How is it going with it?

We have decided to buy a cargo bike, but it is quite expensive. We don't know whether we should borrow the money so we can buy it now, or if we should wait until we have saved up. What would you do?

Once we have bought a cargo bike, maybe we won't need the car at all anymore. I live close to my work, and so does Mette. Do you think we can do without it?

We are also considering skipping traveling by plane for the summer holiday and staying in Denmark. You rented a summer house last year. Do you think that would be something for us? ...

----------------------------------------------------------------

Dear Peter,

Thanks for your email. It was really nice to see you the other day. Regarding our puppy, it is going beyond all expectations. It is growing fast and has already settled well into our home.

How exciting about the cargo bike! If I were in your shoes, I would definitely choose to wait to buy it until you have saved up the money. Even though it can be tempting to borrow, it is always best to avoid unnecessary debt.

As for the car, I am completely convinced that you can easily do without it, since you both live close to your work. A cargo bike is ideal for everyday shopping and transport, so you will definitely save a lot of money on insurance and fuel.

Regarding your summer holiday, I definitely think that a summer house in Denmark would be something for us. The summer house we rented last year was located in an incredibly scenic area, where there was plenty of opportunity to relax. It comes highly recommended, especially if you want a stress-free holiday.

What do you say to us meeting next week over a cup of coffee? Then we can discuss it all more thoroughly, and I can show you some pictures of the summer house.

Best regards,
Emre

DanishWriting:
… Sidst vi var sammen fortalte du, at du tænkte på at tage på en større rejse rundt i Europa. Har du besluttet dig for noget?

Jeg vil lige høre dig om et par ting:
Emma er jo 15 nu og vil gerne have et fritidsjob. Jeg er bare lidt usikker på, om ikke det er for meget, når hun også går til håndbold tre gange om ugen. Hvad tror du?

Hun har også spurgt mig, om hun må tage i sommerhus med sine venner. Vi har jo altid været der også, men nu vil de gerne afsted alene. Er de mon gamle nok til det?

I øvrigt overvejer vi at sælge sommerhuset. Måske kunne vi i stedet bruge pengene på at rejse noget mere, som vi også altid har drømt om. Hvad synes du? …

----------------------------------------------------------------

Kære [Navn],

Tusind tak for din mail. Det var rigtig hyggeligt at høre fra dig, og jeg vil meget gerne dele mine tanker med dig. Hvad angår min rejse til Europa, har jeg faktisk besluttet mig for at tage afsted til august, hvilket jeg glæder mig enormt meget til.

Med hensyn til Emma og et fritidsjob, tror jeg ikke, du behøver at bekymre dig. Selvom hun går til håndbold tre gange om ugen, kan et lille job lære hende at tage ansvar. Det er dog vigtigt, at det kun er et par timer om ugen, så det ikke går ud over hendes skolegang.

Når det kommer til sommerhuset, er det en svær beslutning. Jeg mener godt, at de kan tage afsted alene som 15-årige, så længe I laver nogle klare aftaler på forhånd. På den anden side forstår jeg godt dine overvejelser om at sælge huset. Det lyder som en fantastisk idé at bruge pengene på at rejse og opleve verden, da det altid har været jeres store drøm.

Hvad siger du til, at vi mødes i næste uge over en kop kaffe? Så kan vi vende det hele overgribende og snakke meget mere om jeres planer. Lad mig høre, hvornår det passer dig bedst.

Mange kærlige hilsner,
Emre
EnglishWriting:
... Last time we were together, you mentioned that you were thinking about going on a major trip around Europe. Have you decided on anything?

I just want to ask you about a few things:
Emma is 15 now and wants to get a student job. I'm just a bit unsure if it might be too much, considering she also plays handball three times a week. What do you think?

She also asked me if she could go to the summer house with her friends. We've always been there too, but now they want to go alone. Are they old enough for that, do you think?

By the way, we are considering selling the summer house. Maybe we could use the money to travel more instead, which we've also always dreamed of. What do you think? ...

----------------------------------------------------------------

Dear [Name],

Thank you so much for your email. It was really nice to hear from you, and I would love to share my thoughts with you. As for my trip to Europe, I have actually decided to leave in August, which I am looking forward to immensely.

Regarding Emma and a student job, I don't think you need to worry. Even though she plays handball three times a week, a small job can teach her to take responsibility. However, it is important that it is only a few hours a week so it doesn't affect her schooling.

When it comes to the summer house, it is a tough decision. I do think they can go there alone at 15, as long as you make some clear agreements in advance. On the other hand, I completely understand your reflections on selling the house. It sounds like a fantastic idea to use the money to travel and experience the world, as that has always been your big dream.

How about we meet up next week over a cup of coffee? Then we can discuss everything thoroughly and talk much more about your plans. Let me know when it suits you best.

Warm regards,
Emre

DanishWriting:
… Hvor er det spændende, at du har fået nyt arbejde. Hvordan har det været at starte?

Viggo fylder jo 10 år i næste måned, og han ønsker sig en mobiltelefon. Synes du, det er for tidligt at få en telefon?

Han går allerede til fodbold og svømning, men nu vil han også gerne starte til spejder. Tror du ikke, det bliver for meget for ham?

Jeg tænker også selv på at starte på noget motion. Synes du, jeg skal melde mig ind i et fitnesscenter eller for eksempel begynde at spille badminton? …

----------------------------------------------------------------

Kære [Navn],

Tusind tak for din mail. Det var rigtig hyggeligt at høre fra dig, og jeg vil meget gerne svare på dine spørgsmål.

Først og fremmest tak, fordi du spørger til mit nye arbejde! Det har været utrolig spændende, men også en smule overvældende at starte. Mine nye kolleger er heldigvis meget søde og hjælpsomme, så jeg er allerede rigtig glad for det.

Hvad angår Viggo og mobiltelefonen, så tror jeg faktisk ikke, det er for tidligt. Da han fylder 10 år, har han nok brug for at kunne komme i kontakt med jer efter skole. Det giver en stor tryghed i hverdagen.

Med hensyn til hans fritidsaktiviteter, vil jeg dog råde dig til at passe lidt på. Selvom det er dejligt, at han har masser af energi, tror jeg desværre, at det bliver for meget for ham med tre forskellige aktiviteter ved siden af skolen. Det er vigtigt, at han også har tid til at slappe af og lave lektier.

Når det kommer til din egen motion, vil jeg helt klart anbefale dig at begynde til badminton. Det er en holdsport, hvilket gør det langt mere socialt og underholdende end at træne alene i et fitnesscenter. Derudover er det nemmere at holde motivationen oppe, når man har en fast aftale med andre.

Hvad siger du til, at vi mødes i næste uge over en kop kaffe? Så kan vi vende det hele og forhåbentlig ses snart.

Kærlig hilsen,
Emre
EnglishWriting:
... How exciting that you have got a new job. How has it been to start?

Viggo is turning 10 next month, as you know, and he wants a mobile phone. Do you think it is too early to get a phone?

He already does football and swimming, but now he would also like to start scouts. Don't you think it will be too much for him?

I am also thinking about starting some exercise myself. Do you think I should join a fitness center or, for example, start playing badminton? ...

----------------------------------------------------------------

Dear [Name],

Thank you so much for your email. It was really nice to hear from you, and I would love to answer your questions.

First of all, thank you for asking about my new job! It has been incredibly exciting, but also a bit overwhelming to start. Fortunately, my new colleagues are very sweet and helpful, so I am already really happy with it.

Regarding Viggo and the mobile phone, I actually don't think it is too early. Since he is turning 10, he probably needs to be able to get in touch with you after school. It provides a great sense of security in everyday life.

With regard to his leisure activities, however, I would advise you to be a little careful. Although it is great that he has lots of energy, I unfortunately think it will be too much for him with three different activities alongside school. It is important that he also has time to relax and do homework.

When it comes to your own exercise, I would definitely recommend that you start playing badminton. It is a team sport, which makes it far more social and entertaining than working out alone in a fitness center. In addition, it is easier to keep the motivation up when you have a fixed appointment with others.

What do you say to us meeting next week over a cup of coffee? Then we can discuss everything and hopefully see each other soon.

Warm regards,
Emre

DanishWriting:
… Tak for sidst, det var hyggeligt. Du sagde, at du snart er færdig med din danskuddannelse, men at du ikke føler, du er god nok til dansk. Har du tænkt mere over, hvad du nu vil gøre?

Jeg kender endnu ikke så mange her i byen, men mange af mine kolleger bor heromkring, og de er egentlig rigtig søde. Tror du, det vil være en god idé at invitere dem alle sammen hjem til mig en aften?

Og så noget helt andet. På min nye arbejdsplads er der kage til både møder og fødselsdage, og det synes jeg simpelthen bliver for meget. Synes du, jeg skal foreslå, at vi dropper kagen til møderne?

Og så har jeg lige et praktisk spørgsmål til dig. Jeg har jo stadig en del af mine ting stående i kælderen under min gamle lejlighed. De skal flyttes herover. Har du mulighed for at hjælpe mig med det i weekenden? …

----------------------------------------------------

Kære [Navn],

Tak for din mail, det var rigtig hyggeligt at høre fra dig.

Hvor var det rart at ses sidst! Angående mine overvejelser om min danskuddannelse, så har jeg faktisk besluttet, at jeg vil melde mig til et aftenkursus, når jeg er færdig. Selvom jeg har lært meget, føler jeg stadig, at jeg har brug for at træne mit mundtlige dansk, så jeg kan blive mere sikker.

Hvad angår dine kolleger, lyder det som en fantastisk idé at invitere dem hjem til dig. Da du er ny i byen, er det en oplagt mulighed for at lære dem bedre at kende i uformelle rammer, hvilket helt sikkert vil styrke jeres relationer.

Når det kommer til kagen på din arbejdsplads, kan jeg godt forstå din frustration. Det kan dog være lidt følsomt at foreslå at droppe den helt, da mange sikkert forbinder det med hygge. I stedet for at afskaffe den, kunne du måske foreslå, at I skifter kagen ud med frugt eller sundere snacks til møderne?

Med hensyn til din flytning i weekenden vil jeg utrolig gerne hjælpe dig. Jeg har fri hele lørdagen, så du siger bare til, hvornår det passer dig bedst, at jeg kommer forbi.

Det kunne være superhyggeligt, hvis vi mødtes snart, så vi kan vende det hele over en kop kaffe. Lad mig vide, hvornår du har tid.

De bedste hilsner,
Emre
EnglishWriting:
... Thanks for last time, it was nice. You said that you are finishing your Danish education soon, but that you don't feel you are good enough at Danish. Have you thought more about what you want to do now?

I don't know many people here in town yet, but many of my colleagues live around here, and they are actually really sweet. Do you think it would be a good idea to invite them all over to my place one evening?

And then something completely different. At my new workplace, there is cake for both meetings and birthdays, and I simply think that's too much. Do you think I should suggest that we drop the cake at the meetings?

And then I just have a practical question for you. I still have some of my things standing in the basement under my old apartment. They need to be moved over here. Do you have the opportunity to help me with that this weekend? ...

----------------------------------------------------

Dear [Name],

Thanks for your email, it was really nice to hear from you.

How lovely it was to see you last time! Regarding my thoughts on my Danish education, I have actually decided to enroll in an evening course once I am finished. Although I have learned a lot, I still feel that I need to practice my spoken Danish so I can become more confident.

As for your colleagues, it sounds like a fantastic idea to invite them over to your place. Since you are new in town, it is an obvious opportunity to get to know them better in an informal setting, which will definitely strengthen your relationships.

When it comes to the cake at your workplace, I can well understand your frustration. However, it can be a bit sensitive to suggest dropping it completely, as many people probably associate it with coziness (hygge). Instead of abolishing it, perhaps you could suggest replacing the cake with fruit or healthier snacks for the meetings?

Regarding your move this weekend, I would love to help you. I am free all day Saturday, so just let me know when it suits you best for me to come by.

It would be super nice if we met up soon, so we can talk about everything over a cup of coffee. Let me know when you have time.

Best regards,
Emre

DanishWriting:
... Du snakkede om sidst, at I skulle have nyt køkken. Hvordan er det blevet?

Mathias er jo i gang med at uddanne sig til elektriker. Han får derfor skiftevis SU og lærlingeløn, mens han tager sin uddannelse. Synes du, han burde betale husleje for at bo herhjemme?

Jeg vil egentlig gerne opfordre ham til at læse videre til ingeniør. Tror du, det er en dårlig idé, at jeg blander mig?

Vi plejer jo at tage til Italien i sommerferien. Men Mathias har jo fået en kæreste, som han nu har haft i et halvt års tid. Synes du, vi skal invitere hende med? ...

----------------------------------------------------------------

Kære Hanne,

Mange tak for din mail. Det var rigtig hyggeligt at høre fra dig, og jeg håber, at alt går vel hos jer.

Du spørger først, hvordan det er gået med vores nye køkken. Det er faktisk blevet utrolig flot! Det tog lang tid at renovere, men resultatet er blevet præcis, som vi drømte om, så vi er meget glade for det.

Hvad angår Mathias og husleje, så synes jeg faktisk, det er en god idé, at han betaler et lille beløb for at bo hjemme. Selvom han er i gang med sin uddannelse, lærer det ham at tage ansvar for sin egen økonomi, hvilket er en vigtig erfaring.

Når det gælder dine overvejelser om at opfordre ham til at læse videre til ingeniør, tror jeg bestemt ikke, det er en dårlig idé at blande sig. Som forælder kan man sagtens give gode råd og sparre om fremtiden, så længe du blot præsenterer det som en mulighed og lader det være hans eget valg i sidste ende.

Med hensyn til sommerferien i Italien, vil jeg varmt anbefale dig at invitere hans kæreste med. Da de har været sammen i et halvt år nu, vil det helt sikkert være en god mulighed for jer til at lære hende meget bedre at kende, og det vil helt sikkert gøre Mathias glad.

Hvad siger du til, at vi mødes i den nærmeste fremtid? Så kan vi vende det hele over en kop kaffe. Lad mig vide, hvornår det passer dig bedst.

Kærlig hilsen,
Emre
EnglishWriting:
... You mentioned last time that you were getting a new kitchen. How did it turn out?

Mathias is, as you know, training to be an electrician. He therefore receives a mix of SU (student grant) and apprentice wages while doing his education. Do you think he should pay rent to live at home?

I would actually like to encourage him to study further to become an engineer. Do you think it's a bad idea for me to interfere?

We usually go to Italy for the summer holidays. But Mathias has got a girlfriend, whom he has now been with for about half a year. Do you think we should invite her along? ...

----------------------------------------------------------------

Dear Hanne,

Thank you very much for your email. It was really nice to hear from you, and I hope everything is going well with you.

First, you ask how it went with our new kitchen. It actually turned out incredibly beautiful! It took a long time to renovate, but the result is exactly what we dreamed of, so we are very happy with it.

Regarding Mathias and rent, I actually think it is a good idea for him to pay a small amount to live at home. Even though he is in the middle of his education, it teaches him to take responsibility for his own finances, which is an important experience.

When it comes to your reflections on encouraging him to study further to become an engineer, I certainly don't think it's a bad idea to interfere. As a parent, you can easily give good advice and brainstorm about the future, as long as you just present it as an option and let it be his own choice in the end.

With regard to the summer holiday in Italy, I would warmly recommend that you invite his girlfriend along. Since they have been together for half a year now, it will definitely be a great opportunity for you to get to know her much better, and it will certainly make Mathias happy.

What do you say to us meeting up in the near future? Then we can discuss everything over a cup of coffee. Let me know when suits you best.

Best regards,
Emre

DanishWriting:
… Tak for sidst, det var hyggeligt at mødes med dig. Du sagde, at du leder efter et nyt sted at bo. Hvordan går det med det?

Som sagt, så glæder vi os til, at vi til vinter skal være forældre for første gang. Vi har stadig ikke besluttet, om vi vil have barnets køn at vide før fødslen. Synes du, man skal vide på forhånd, om det bliver en pige eller en dreng?

Vi har snakket om, at vi vil tage en måneds forældreorlov sammen og så rejse en tur ud i verden med den lille ny. Synes du ikke, det lyder som en god idé?

Du var jo med os på ferie sidste år, og det var så hyggeligt. I år tager vi en uge op i mine forældres sommerhus i slutningen af juli. Vil du med? …

----------------------------------------------------------------

Kære [Navn],

Tusind tak for din mail, som jeg blev rigtig glad for at modtage. Det var i øvrigt superhyggeligt at ses sidst!

Hvad angår min boligsituation, så går det faktisk ret godt. Efter at have ledt i et stykke tid har jeg endelig fundet en hyggelig lejlighed i nærheden af mit arbejde, som jeg overtager i næste måned.

Hvor er det spændende med den lille ny! Mht. jeres overvejelser om barnets køn, vil jeg personligt anbefale, at I venter med at få det at vide til fødslen. Det må være en helt ubeskrivelig oplevelse at få overraskelsen i selve øjeblikket.

Jeres rejseplaner lyder fuldstændig fantastiske i mine ører. Det er en unik mulighed for jer som nybagte forældre til at knytte stærke bånd til hinanden i rolige omgivelser, før hverdagen for alvor rammer.

Tusind tak for den dejlige invitation til sommerhuset i slutningen af juli! Jeg vil utrolig gerne med, da jeg har gode minder fra vores ferie sidste år.

Det kunne være superhyggeligt, hvis vi kunne mødes en af dagene, så vi kan vende det hele over en kop kaffe. Lad mig vide, hvornår det passer dig bedst.

De bedste hilsner,
Emre
EnglishWriting:
... Thanks for the last time, it was nice meeting you. You said that you are looking for a new place to live. How is that going?

As mentioned, we are looking forward to becoming parents for the first time this winter. We still haven't decided whether we want to know the baby's gender before the birth. Do you think one should know in advance whether it will be a girl or a boy?

We have talked about taking a month of parental leave together and then traveling the world with the little new one. Don't you think that sounds like a good idea?

You were with us on holiday last year, and it was so cozy. This year, we are spending a week up in my parents' summer house at the end of July. Do you want to come along? ...

----------------------------------------------------------------

Dear [Name],

Thank you so much for your email, which I was really glad to receive. By the way, it was super cozy to see each other last time!

Regarding my housing situation, it is actually going quite well. After searching for a while, I have finally found a cozy apartment near my work, which I will take over next month.

How exciting it is with the little new one! Regarding your reflections on the baby's gender, I would personally recommend that you wait to find out until the birth. It must be an absolutely indescribable experience to get the surprise in the exact moment.

Your travel plans sound completely fantastic to my ears. It is a unique opportunity for you as new parents to form strong bonds with each other in peaceful surroundings before everyday life truly hits.

Thank you so much for the lovely invitation to the summer house at the end of July! I would incredibly love to come along, as I have great memories from our holiday last year.

It would be super cozy if we could meet up one of these days so we can discuss it all over a cup of coffee. Let me know when it suits you best.

Best regards,
Emre

DanishWriting:
… Tak for sidst. Jeg håber, du havde en god ferie. Hvordan var det så at vandre i bjergene?

Jeg er rigtig glad for mit nye job. Jeg har dog det problem, at der er meget larm på kontoret, hvor jeg arbejder. Vi sidder over 20 personer i samme rum, og især to af mine kolleger snakker meget sammen. Jeg overvejer at sige til dem, at det forstyrrer mig. Synes du, jeg skal gøre det?

Mange af mine nye kolleger bliver tit en time eller to efter arbejde om fredagen og drikker en øl eller sodavand. Jeg vil egentlig helst bare hjem og holde weekend. Tror du, det er vigtigt, at jeg alligevel bliver?

Vi skal jo snart til Steens 30-års fødselsdag og har aftalt at give en gave sammen. Men hvad skal vi egentlig give ham? …

----------------------------------------------------------------

Kære Claus,

Tak for din mail. Det var rigtig hyggeligt at se dig sidst. Min ferie var helt fantastisk, og det var en stor oplevelse at vandre i bjergene, selvom det var ret hårdt for benene.

Hvor er det dejligt at høre, at du er glad for dit nye arbejde! Det er dog utroligt ærgerligt med larmen på kontoret. Hvis jeg var dig, ville jeg helt klart sige det til de to kolleger, men på en pæn og høflig måde. Det er vigtigt med arbejdsro, og de tænker sikkert ikke over, at de forstyrrer dig.

Hvad angår fredagsøllen, kan jeg godt forstå, at du helst bare vil hjem. Men i starten tror jeg faktisk, det er ret vigtigt, at du bliver en gang imellem. Det er en rigtig god måde at lære dine nye kolleger bedre at kende på og vise, at du gerne vil være en del af fællesskabet. Du behøver jo ikke at blive i flere timer hver fredag.

Med hensyn til gaven til Steens 30-års fødselsdag, har jeg tænkt på, at vi måske kunne give ham et gavekort til en god restaurant eller en oplevelse, da han jo elsker god mad. Hvad tænker du om det?

Vi bliver nødt til at mødes snart, så vi kan aftale detaljerne og få vendt det hele over en kop kaffe. Passer det dig i næste uge?

Kærlig hilsen,
Emre
EnglishWriting:
... Thanks for the last time we met. I hope you had a good vacation. How was it hiking in the mountains?

I am really happy with my new job. However, I have the problem that there is a lot of noise in the office where I work. We are over 20 people in the same room, and especially two of my colleagues talk a lot together. I am considering telling them that it disturbs me. Do you think I should do it?

Many of my new colleagues often stay for an hour or two after work on Fridays and drink a beer or soda. I would actually rather just go home and start the weekend. Do you think it is important that I stay anyway?

We are soon going to Steen's 30th birthday and have agreed to give a gift together. But what should we actually give him? ...

----------------------------------------------------------------

Dear Claus,

Thanks for your email. It was really nice to see you last time. My vacation was absolutely fantastic, and it was a great experience hiking in the mountains, even though it was quite hard on the legs.

How wonderful to hear that you are happy with your new job! However, it is incredibly a shame about the noise in the office. If I were you, I would definitely tell the two colleagues, but in a nice and polite way. Quiet surroundings are important for working, and they probably don't realize that they are disturbing you.

Regarding the Friday beer, I can well understand that you would rather just go home. But in the beginning, I actually think it is quite important that you stay once in a while. It is a really good way to get to know your new colleagues better and show that you want to be a part of the community. You don't have to stay for several hours every Friday.

With regard to the gift for Steen's 30th birthday, I have been thinking that we could perhaps give him a gift card for a good restaurant or an experience, since he loves good food. What do you think about that?

We need to meet up soon so we can agree on the details and catch up on everything over a cup of coffee. Does next week suit you?

Warm regards,
Emre

DanishWriting:
… Tak for snakken i sidste uge, det var hyggeligt at tale med dig på Skype. Jeg er meget spændt på at høre om din køreprøve. Hvordan gik det?

Som du ved, er vores tid i New York snart slut, og vi skal hjem til Odense om tre uger. Jeg skal arbejde sammen med nogle kolleger, jeg ikke kender. Hvad synes du, jeg skal gøre for at få en god start på arbejdet?

Børnene er jo blevet teenagere, og de er meget kede af at sige farvel til deres venner her i New York. Jeg er spændt på, om de kan holde kontakten. Tror du, det er muligt?

Jens har fået nyt arbejde i Danmark, som han glæder sig til at komme i gang med efter 3 år som hjemmegående. Det nye job ligger dog 30 km fra Odense, så vi overvejer at købe en ekstra bil, men han kan også tage toget. Hvad ville du gøre?

----------------------------------------------------

Kære Mia,

Tusind tak for din mail. Det var utroligt hyggeligt at tale med dig på Skype i sidste uge, og det glæder mig altid at høre fra dig.

For det første vil jeg skynde mig at fortælle, at min køreprøve desværre ikke gik helt som forventet, da jeg dumpede på grund af en lille fejl. Selvom det er super ærgerligt, har jeg allerede bestilt en ny tid, og jeg satser stærkt på, at det lykkes næste gang.

Hvad angår din situation på det nye arbejde, kan jeg godt forstå, at du er spændt. Hvis jeg var dig, ville jeg sørge for at være åben og tage initiativ til at spise frokost med de nye kolleger i de første par uger. Det plejer at være en rigtig god måde at bryde isen på.

Når det kommer til børnene, er det fuldstændig forståeligt, at de er kede af at skulle sige farvel. Men i dag er det heldigvis utroligt nemt at holde kontakten via de sociale medier og FaceTime, så jeg er slet ikke i tvivl om, at det er muligt for dem at bevare venskaberne på tværs af Atlanten.

Med hensyn til Jens’ transportsituation, er det et godt spørgsmål. Hvis jeg stod i jeres situation, ville jeg helt klart anbefale ham at tage toget i begyndelsen. På den måde slipper han for stress i morgentrafikken, og han kan bruge transporttiden på enten at slappe af eller forberede sig til arbejdet. Hvis det viser sig at være for besværligt, kan I altid købe en ekstra bil senere.

Nu hvor I snart flytter tilbage til Danmark, kunne det være super hyggeligt, hvis vi mødtes til en kop kaffe i Odense. Lad mig endelig vide, hvornår I har tid og overskud i kalenderen.

Hils familien mange gange.

Kærlig hilsen,
Emre
EnglishWriting:
… Thanks for the chat last week, it was nice talking to you on Skype. I am very excited to hear about your driving test. How did it go?

As you know, our time in New York is soon coming to an end, and we are going home to Odense in three weeks. I am going to work with some colleagues I don’t know. What do you think I should do to get a good start at work?

The children have become teenagers now, and they are very sad to say goodbye to their friends here in New York. I am excited to see if they can keep in touch. Do you think that is possible?

Jens has got a new job in Denmark, which he is looking forward to getting started with after 3 years as a stay-at-home dad. However, the new job is located 30 km from Odense, so we are considering buying an extra car, but he could also take the train. What would you do?

----------------------------------------------------

Dear Mia,

Thank you so much for your email. It was incredibly nice talking to you on Skype last week, and it always pleases me to hear from you.

Firstly, I will hasten to tell you that my driving test unfortunately did not go quite as expected, as I failed due to a small mistake. Although it is super annoying, I have already booked a new appointment, and I strongly bet on succeeding next time.

As for your situation at the new job, I can well understand that you are excited. If I were you, I would make sure to be open and take the initiative to eat lunch with your new colleagues during the first couple of weeks. That is usually a really good way to break the ice.

When it comes to the children, it is completely understandable that they are sad about having to say goodbye. However, nowadays it is fortunately incredibly easy to keep in touch via social media and FaceTime, so I have no doubt at all that it is possible for them to maintain their friendships across the Atlantic.

Regarding Jens' transport situation, that is a good question. If I were in your situation, I would definitely recommend him to take the train in the beginning. That way, he avoids stress in the morning traffic, and he can use the travel time to either relax or prepare for work. If it turns out to be too inconvenient, you can always buy an extra car later.

Now that you are soon moving back to Denmark, it would be super nice if we met up for a cup of coffee in Odense. Please let me know when you have time and energy in your calendar.

Give my best to the family.

Warm regards,
Emre

DanishWriting:
Diagrammet viser andelen af den voksne danske befolkning, der udførte frivilligt arbejde i 2020 fordelt på forskellige områder. Det fremgår tydeligt, at idrætsområdet er det mest populære valg, da over 12% af befolkningen er aktive her. Herefter følger det sociale område med cirka 5%, mens kulturområdet ligger på omkring 4%. Derimod er miljøområdet det mindst udbredte med kun omkring 1%.

Der kan være flere årsager til disse markante forskelle. For det første har Danmark en stærk tradition for foreningsliv, især inden for sport, hvor forældre ofte hjælper til i deres børns klubber. Det er en naturlig del af det sociale liv. For det andet er det sociale område (f.eks. besøgsvenner) meget synligt i medierne, hvilket kan motivere folk til at hjælpe sårbare medborgere. Miljøområdet kræver måske mere specifik viden eller er mindre præget af direkte social kontakt, hvilket kan forklare den lave deltagelse.

Spørgsmålet om, hvorvidt manglen på sygeplejersker og social- og sundhedsassistenter kan løses med frivillig arbejdskraft, er komplekst. På den ene side kan frivillige bidrage med værdifuld menneskelig kontakt, såsom at gå ture med de ældre eller læse højt, hvilket kan aflaste personalet for de bløde opgaver.

På den anden side er det vigtigt at understrege, at frivillige aldrig kan erstatte faglært arbejdskraft. Arbejdet som sygeplejerske kræver en lang uddannelse og indebærer et stort medicinsk ansvar. Hvis man overlader kerneopgaver til frivillige, kan det gå ud over patientsikkerheden. Desuden er frivilligt arbejde, som navnet antyder, uforpligtende. En stabil drift af et sygehus kræver medarbejdere, der er ansat på faste kontrakter.

Sammenfattende mener jeg, at frivillige kan være et godt supplement til sundhedsvæsenet for at øge livskvaliteten hos patienterne, men selve manglen på arbejdskraft skal løses gennem bedre arbejdsvilkår og uddannelse af professionelt personale.
EnglishWriting:
The diagram shows the proportion of the adult Danish population who carried out volunteer work in 2020, distributed across different areas. It is clear that the sports area is the most popular choice, as over 12% of the population is active here. This is followed by the social area with around 5%, while the cultural area is around 4%. On the other hand, the environmental area is the least widespread with only around 1%.

There may be several reasons for these significant differences. Firstly, Denmark has a strong tradition of association life, especially within sports, where parents often help out in their children's clubs. It is a natural part of social life. Secondly, the social area (e.g. visiting friends) is very visible in the media, which can motivate people to help vulnerable citizens. The environmental area may require more specific knowledge or is less characterized by direct social contact, which may explain the low participation.

The question of whether the shortage of nurses and social and health assistants can be solved with volunteer labor is complex. On the one hand, volunteers can contribute with valuable human contact, such as walking with the elderly or reading aloud, which can relieve staff of the soft tasks.

On the other hand, it is important to emphasize that volunteers can never replace skilled labor. The work of a nurse requires a long education and involves a great deal of medical responsibility. If core tasks are left to volunteers, patient safety may be compromised. Furthermore, volunteer work, as the name suggests, is non-binding. Stable operation of a hospital requires employees who are employed on permanent contracts.

Conclusion In summary, I believe that volunteers can be a good supplement to the healthcare system in order to increase the quality of life of patients, but the shortage of labor itself must be solved through better working conditions and training of professional personnel.

DanishWriting:
Diagrammet viser andelen af personer i forskellige aldersgrupper, der er meget bekymrede over klimaforandringer. Det fremgår tydeligt, at den yngste aldersgruppe (18-34 år) er den mest bekymrede, da omkring 27% af personerne i denne gruppe giver udtryk for stor bekymring. Herefter følger gruppen på 35-49 år med cirka 24%, mens den ældste gruppe (50-70 år) ligger lavest med omkring 22%. For hele befolkningen samlet set er tallet cirka 24%.

Der kan være flere årsager til disse markante forskelle. For det første er det de unge, der skal leve længst med konsekvenserne af klimaforandringerne, hvilket naturligt skaber en større frygt for fremtiden. For det andet fylder klimadebatten meget i uddannelsessystemet og på de sociale medier, hvor de unge primært henter deres informationer. De ældre generationer er måske mere præget af andre dagsordener eller har en større tillid til, at teknologiske løsninger nok skal løse problemerne hen ad vejen.

Spørgsmålet om, hvilke konsekvenser de unges klimabekymring har for samfundet, er komplekst. På den ene side er det en stor fordel, at de unge er engagerede og presser politikerne til at tage grønne beslutninger. Deres bekymring fungerer som en motor for innovation og nødvendige adfærdsændringer, hvilket er afgørende for den grønne omstilling.

På den anden side er det vigtigt at understrege, at en ekstrem bekymring også kan have ulemper. Hvis bekymringen udvikler sig til decideret "klima-angst", kan det gå ud over de unges mentale trivsel og føre til handlingslammelse frem for engagement. Desuden kan det skabe en dyb generationskløft, hvis de unge føler, at de ældre generationer slet ikke tager deres bekymringer alvorligt.

Sammenfattende mener jeg, at de unges bekymring er et positivt supplement til den politiske debat, da den skaber handling. Dog skal samfundet sørge for, at bekymringen bliver vendt til konstruktive løsninger, så den ikke ender med at blive en belastning for de unges livskvalitet.
EnglishWriting:
The chart shows the proportion of people in different age groups who are very concerned about climate change. It is clear that the youngest age group (18-34 years) is the most concerned, as around 27% of people in this group express great concern. This is followed by the 35-49 year group with around 24%, while the oldest group (50-70 years) is the lowest with around 22%. For the entire population as a whole, the figure is around 24%.

There may be several reasons for these significant differences. Firstly, it is the young who will have to live with the consequences of climate change the longest, which naturally creates greater fear for the future. Secondly, the climate debate occupies a large part of the education system and on social media, where young people primarily get their information. Older generations may be more influenced by other agendas or have greater confidence that technological solutions will eventually solve the problems.

The question of what consequences young people's climate concerns have for society is complex. On the one hand, it is a great advantage that young people are committed and pressure politicians to make green decisions. Their concerns act as a driver for innovation and necessary behavioral changes, which is crucial for the green transition.

On the other hand, it is important to emphasize that extreme concern can also have disadvantages. If the concern develops into outright "climate anxiety", it can affect the mental well-being of young people and lead to paralysis of action rather than commitment. Furthermore, it can create a deep generation gap if young people feel that older generations do not take their concerns seriously at all.

In summary, I believe that young people's concerns are a positive supplement to the political debate, as they create action. However, society must ensure that concerns are turned into constructive solutions so that they do not end up becoming a burden on the quality of life of young people.

DanishWritingTitle: 2A: Jobomsætning i Danmark fordelt på alder
DanishWriting:
Diagrammet viser antallet af personer i Danmark, der skiftede job i 2018, fordelt på forskellige aldersgrupper. Det fremgår tydeligt, at den yngste aldersgruppe (20-34 år) er den mest mobile på arbejdsmarkedet, da over 40.000 personer i denne gruppe skiftede job. Herefter følger gruppen på 35-49 år med cirka 23.000, mens den ældste gruppe (50-64 år) ligger lavest med omkring 14.000 jobskifter.

Der kan være flere årsager til disse markante forskelle. For det første er de unge ofte i en fase af livet, hvor de afprøver forskellige karriereveje og søger nye udfordringer for at stige i løn eller få mere ansvar. For det andet har de ældre generationer ofte etableret sig med familie, fast bopæl og længere anciennitet, hvilket gør dem mere tilbøjelige til at blive i den samme stilling. De prioriterer måske stabilitet og tryghed højere end de yngre generationer.

Spørgsmålet om, hvilke konsekvenser en løbende udskiftning af medarbejdere har for en virksomhed, er komplekst. På den ene side er det en stor fordel, da nye medarbejdere ofte bringer friske øjne, ny viden og fornyet energi til arbejdspladsen. En vis grad af udskiftning fungerer som en motor for innovation og forhindrer, at virksomheden går i stå i gamle rutiner.

På den anden side er det vigtigt at understrege, at en for høj jobomsætning også har klare ulemper. Det er dyrt og tidskrævende at rekruttere og oplære nye folk, hvilket kan føre til et tab af produktivitet. Desuden kan det skabe uro i de eksisterende teams og medføre tab af vigtig "know-how" og erfaring, hvis for mange nøglepersoner forlader virksomheden samtidig.

Sammenfattende mener jeg, at en moderat udskiftning af medarbejdere er et sundt supplement til en virksomheds udvikling. Dog skal ledelsen sørge for at skabe en balance, så man både tiltrækker nye talenter og samtidig formår at fastholde de erfarne kræfter, der sikrer kontinuitet i arbejdet.
EnglishWritingTitle: 2A: Job turnover in Denmark by age
EnglishWriting:
The chart shows the number of people in Denmark who changed jobs in 2018, divided into different age groups. It is clear that the youngest age group (20-34 years) is the most mobile in the labor market, as over 40,000 people in this group changed jobs. This is followed by the 35-49 year group with approximately 23,000, while the oldest group (50-64 years) is the lowest with approximately 14,000 job changes.

There may be several reasons for these significant differences. Firstly, young people are often in a phase of life where they are trying out different career paths and looking for new challenges in order to increase their salary or get more responsibility. Secondly, the older generations are often established with family, permanent residence and longer seniority, which makes them more likely to stay in the same position. They may prioritize stability and security more than younger generations.

The question of what consequences a continuous turnover of employees has for a company is complex. On the one hand, it is a great advantage, as new employees often bring fresh eyes, new knowledge and renewed energy to the workplace. A certain degree of turnover acts as an engine for innovation and prevents the company from getting stuck in old routines.

On the other hand, it is important to emphasize that too high a job turnover also has clear disadvantages. Recruiting and training new people is expensive and time-consuming, which can lead to a loss of productivity. Furthermore, it can create unrest in existing teams and lead to the loss of important "know-how" and experience if too many key people leave the company at the same time.

In summary, I believe that a moderate turnover of employees is a healthy supplement to a company's development. However, management must ensure that a balance is created so that new talent is attracted while at the same time retaining experienced staff to ensure continuity in the work.

DanishWriting:
Diagrammet viser det gennemsnitlige sygefravær i procent inden for fire udvalgte jobtyper. Det fremgår tydeligt, at der er store forskelle mellem faggrupperne. Social- og sundhedsassistenter har det højeste fravær på over 7%, efterfulgt af sygeplejersker med cirka 5,5%. I den anden ende af skalaen finder man forskere ved universiteter med omkring 2% og ledere, som har det laveste sygefravær på cirka 1,5%.

Der kan være flere årsager til disse markante forskelle. For det første har social- og sundhedsassistenter samt sygeplejersker ofte et meget fysisk krævende arbejde med tunge løft og et højt tempo, hvilket øger risikoen for både skader og nedslidning. For det andet er disse personalegrupper i tæt kontakt med syge mennesker, hvilket gør dem mere udsatte for smitte. Ledere og forskere har derimod ofte mere fleksible arbejdsforhold og kontorarbejde, som ikke belaster kroppen på samme måde. De har måske også en højere grad af indflydelse på deres egen arbejdsdag, hvilket kan mindske stress.

Spørgsmålet om, hvorvidt det er ledelsens eller medarbejdernes eget ansvar at nedbringe sygefraværet, er komplekst. På den ene side har ledelsen et stort ansvar for at sikre et sundt arbejdsmiljø. Det er ledelsens opgave at sørge for tilstrækkelig bemanding, de rette hjælpemidler og et godt psykisk arbejdsklima, så medarbejderne ikke bliver syge af stress eller overbelastning.

På den anden side er det vigtigt at understrege, at medarbejderne også selv har et ansvar for deres sundhed. Den enkelte medarbejder bør så vidt muligt forsøge at leve sundt og sige fra, hvis arbejdspresset bliver for stort. Desuden hviler der et ansvar på den enkelte for at bidrage positivt til fællesskabet på arbejdspladsen, da trivsel ofte hænger sammen med det sociale sammenhold.

Sammenfattende mener jeg, at en indsats fra medarbejderne kan fungere som et godt supplement, men det overordnede ansvar ligger hos ledelsen. Det kræver en fælles dialog at skabe en arbejdsplads, hvor alle trives, og hvor sygefraværet kan holdes på et lavt niveau.
EnglishWritingTitle: 2A: Sickness absence within different job types
EnglishWriting:
The diagram shows the average sick leave in percentage within four selected job types. It is clear that there are large differences between the professional groups. Social and health assistants have the highest absence of over 7%, followed by nurses with approximately 5.5%. At the other end of the scale, you find researchers at universities with around 2% and managers, who have the lowest sick leave of approximately 1.5%.

There may be several reasons for these significant differences. Firstly, social and health assistants and nurses often have very physically demanding work with heavy lifting and a fast pace, which increases the risk of both injuries and burnout. Secondly, these staff groups are in close contact with sick people, which makes them more exposed to infection. Managers and researchers, on the other hand, often have more flexible working conditions and office work, which does not strain the body in the same way. They may also have a greater degree of influence over their own working day, which can reduce stress.

The question of whether it is management's or employees' own responsibility to reduce sickness absence is complex. On the one hand, management has a great responsibility for ensuring a healthy working environment. It is management's task to ensure sufficient staffing, the right aids and a good psychological working climate so that employees do not become ill from stress or overload.

On the other hand, it is important to emphasize that employees also have a responsibility for their own health. Each employee should try to live a healthy life as much as possible and speak up if the work pressure becomes too great. In addition, the individual has a responsibility to contribute positively to the community at the workplace, as well-being is often linked to social cohesion.

In summary, I believe that efforts from employees can function as a good supplement, but the overall responsibility lies with management. It requires a joint dialogue to create a workplace where everyone thrives and where sick leave can be kept at a low level.
